target.name: Clippership
target.founder: Nico Cymbalist, Luca Cymbalist, Kai Matsuka

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Clippership is a seed-stage ocean and maritime technology company that has raised $5M+. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of five private peers in the maritime and subsea sector—Standard Subsea, NetworkOcean, Thalassa Robotics, Albacore, and Odin Dynamics—with no public comparables available for direct benchmarking.

The stage-progression method is the applicable triangulation approach given data availability. Using the $5M total raised as a proxy for current implied valuation and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $12M. This serves as the median anchor point in the analysis.

The triangulated valuation range is $11M to $14M, with $12M as the median. The low end of $11M assumes a more conservative step-up applied to the raised capital base, while the high end of $14M incorporates upside scenarios consistent with stronger stage progression outcomes. Key uncertainties limiting precision include reliance on a single methodological leg—the absence of public comps and limited public disclosure from the private peer set restricts validation through multiple independent approaches. Revenue scale, customer concentration, and near-term milestone achievement remain critical variables that would refine this range materially.
